title: Semantic Analogies
content:
# Interview Prep: Analogies

## Q1: Why does the 3CosMul formulation perform better than 3CosAdd for solving analogies in word embedding spaces?

### Standard Answer
The 3CosAdd formulation evaluates similarity using linear addition:

$$\arg\max_{\mathbf{w}} \left[ \text{sim}(\mathbf{w}, \mathbf{v}_b) - \text{sim}(\mathbf{w}, \mathbf{v}_a) + \text{sim}(\mathbf{w}, \mathbf{v}_c) \right]$$

This additive combination allows a word that is extremely close to $\mathbf{v}_c$ to dominate the optimization, even if it has no relation to the query. For example, in the analogy 'man:king :: apple:?', the model might output 'apples' simply because 'apples' is highly similar to 'apple'.

3CosMul converts this relationship to a product of ratios:

$$\arg\max_{\mathbf{w}} \frac{\text{sim}(\mathbf{w}, \mathbf{v}_b) \times \text{sim}(\mathbf{w}, \mathbf{v}_c)}{\text{sim}(\mathbf{w}, \mathbf{v}_a) + \epsilon}$$

This multiplicative combination acts as an AND gate. A candidate must be similar to both $b$ and $c$ while being distinct from $a$, preventing single-term dominance.
